title: Ledger Run
subtitle: AI Email Invoice Ingestion & Matching
narration: Ledger Run is an A I first invoice processing assistant for clinical trials. Invoices arrive over email, the A I extracts them, matches their line items to the scoped catalog, and decides on its own whether to submit them or hold them for review.

title: The flow, in four stages
bullets: Extract — Claude pulls metadata and line items from the P D F.
Resolve — sponsor, study, and site, via the M C P-wrapped reference A P I.
Match — each line against the sponsor and study scoped catalog.
Decide — a deterministic policy yields SUBMIT or HOLD with reasons.
No human gate before the verdict. Q C happens after, in the hub.
narration: Every invoice runs through extract, resolve, match, and decide. Context resolution and catalog reads go through a real M C P server. The submit-or-hold verdict is a deterministic policy, not the L L M — so every decision is testable and explainable.

title: The hub — submitted vs held lanes
caption: Two SUBMITTED. Six HELD. The trusted stream on the left, the exceptions that need judgment on the right.
narration: This is the hub. Two invoices auto-submitted overnight — the reviewer ignores those. Six are held with exceptions, and that's where the work is. The home page makes the split unmissable.

title: Auto-submit — the simple invoice
caption: Context resolved, totals reconciled, all five line items matched. Even "Complete Blood Count" → catalog "CBC".
narration: Here's the clean path. The simple invoice resolved to Contoso CATALYST at ninety-nine percent confidence, the totals reconcile, and all five line items match. Notice line four: "Complete Blood Count" matched the catalog entry "CBC" — the A I handled the acronym, no token overlap needed.

title: Hold for review — price mismatch
caption: Ten of eleven lines matched. One line's unit price deviates beyond tolerance — HOLD.
narration: The medium invoice resolved cleanly and ten of eleven lines matched. But line ten — I R B Ethics Fees — was billed beyond the price tolerance for its catalog item, so the policy holds the invoice and surfaces exactly which line and why.

title: Large invoice — multiple exceptions on a 100-item catalog
caption: Twenty-seven line items, heavy rewording, abbreviations, and pass-through items. The shortlist keeps the prompt bounded.
narration: The large invoice stresses the matcher. Twenty seven line items against a hundred-item catalog. The shortlist pre-filter keeps the prompt bounded, and the policy ranks every exception — unmatched lines, a low-confidence match, and the reasons appear inline so a reviewer can triage them at a glance.

title: Mismatched metadata — corrected, then held
caption: Sponsor name was wrong. Protocol number won. resolved_corrected → HOLD pending confirmation.
narration: This invoice had wrong metadata — wrong sponsor name, wrong protocol year. The resolver reconciled it by trusting the protocol number over the sponsor name, marked the context as resolved-corrected, and held the invoice so a human can confirm the correction before it ships.

title: What ships
bullets: Real M C P server (six tools) over the s t d i o transport.
Pluggable ingestion — drop-folder, upload, .eml file, real I M A P.
Seventy-two offline tests; clean docker compose up; live demo proven on all four samples.
Decision policy is pure, deterministic, and unit-tested — not the L L M.
narration: Everything ships in one docker compose up. Real M C P over s t d i o, four ingestion sources including a real I M A P mailbox, seventy-two offline tests, and a pure deterministic decision policy. The L L M extracts and matches; the policy decides. That's what makes every verdict explainable.

title: Thanks
subtitle: Ledger Run — code, docs, and tests at the repo root
kicker: AI decides first · you review after
